segments.text: ADAM: So, Michelle, shall we make a start on our presentation?
segments.translation: 亚当：那么，米歇尔，我们可以开始准备演讲了吗？

segments.text: We haven't got that much time left.
segments.translation: 我们没有那么多时间了。

segments.text: MICHELLE: No, Adam. But at least we've done all the background reading.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：是的，亚当。但至少我们已经完成了所有背景阅读。

segments.text: I found it really interesting - I'd never even heard of the Laki eruption before this.
segments.translation: 我发现这真的很有趣。在此之前我从未听说过拉基火山喷发。

segments.text: ADAM: Me neither.
segments.translation: 亚当：我也没有。

segments.text: I suppose 1783 is a long time ago.
segments.translation: 我想1783年是很久以前了。

segments.text: MICHELLE: But it was a huge eruption and it had such devastating consequences.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：但那是一次巨大的喷发，造成了毁灭性的后果。

segments.text: ADAM: I know.
segments.translation: 亚当：我知道。

segments.text: It was great there were so many primary sources to look at.
segments.translation: 有这么多原始资源可供查看很方便。

segments.text: It really gives you a sense of how catastrophic the volcano was.
segments.translation: 它真的让你感受到火山的灾难程度。

segments.text: People were really trying to make sense of the science for the first time.
segments.translation: 人们第一次真正地试图理解科学。

segments.text: MICHELLE: That's right.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：没错。

segments.text: But what I found more significant was how it impacted directly and indirectly on political events, as well as having massive social and economic consequences.
segments.translation: 但我发现更重要的是它如何直接和间接地影响政治事件，以及产生巨大的社会和经济后果。

segments.text: ADAM: I know. That should be the main focus of our presentation.
segments.translation: 亚当：我知道。这应该是我们演讲的重点。

segments.text: MICHELLE: The observations made by people at the time were interesting, weren't they?
segments.translation: 米歇尔：当时人们的观察很有趣，不是吗？

segments.text: I mean, they all gave a pretty consistent account of what happened, even if they didn't always use the same terminology.
segments.translation: 我的意思是，他们都对发生的事情给出了非常一致的描述，即使他们使用的术语不尽相同。

segments.text: ADAM: Yeah. I was surprised there were so many weather stations established by that time - so, you know, you can see how the weather changed, often by the hour.
segments.translation: 亚当：没错。我很惊讶那个时候建立了这么多气象站。所以，你知道，你能看到天气是如何变化的，通常是一小时一个样。

segments.text: MICHELLE: Right. Writers at the time talked about the Laki haze to describe the volcanic fog that spread across Europe.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：对。当时的作家们用拉基雾霾来形容席卷欧洲的火山雾。

segments.text: They all realised that this wasn't the sort of fog they were used to - and of course this was in pre-industrial times - so they hadn't experienced sulphur-smelling fog before.
segments.translation: 他们都意识到这不是他们习惯的那种雾，当然那是在前工业时代，所以他们以前没有感受过硫磺味的雾。

segments.text: ADAM: No, that's true.
segments.translation: 亚当：是的，的确没见过。

segments.text: MICHELLE: Reports from the period blamed the haze for an increase in headaches, respiratory issues and asthma attacks.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：该时期的报告将头痛、呼吸系统问题和哮喘发作的增加归咎于雾霾。

segments.text: And they all describe how it covered the sun and made it look a strange red colour.
segments.translation: 他们都描述了它如何遮住太阳，使它看起来是一种奇怪的红色。

segments.text: ADAM: Must have been very weird.
segments.translation: 亚当：当时一定很奇怪。

segments.text: MICHELLE: It's interesting that Benjamin Franklin wrote about the haze.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：有趣的是，本杰明·富兰克林写了关于雾霾的文章。

segments.text: Did you read that?
segments.translation: 你读过吗？

segments.text: He was the American ambassador in Paris at the time.
segments.translation: 他当时是美国驻巴黎大使。

segments.text: ADAM: Yeah. At first no one realised that the haze was caused by the volcanic eruption in Iceland.
segments.translation: 亚当：是的。起初没有人意识到雾霾是由冰岛火山喷发造成的。

segments.text: MICHELLE: It was Benjamin Franklin who realised that before anyone else.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：本杰明·富兰克林比任何人都先意识到这一点。

segments.text: ADAM: He's often credited with that, apparently.
segments.translation: 亚当：显然，这经常被认为是他的功劳。

segments.text: But a French naturalist beat him to it - I can't remember his name.
segments.translation: 但一位法国博物学家抢在他之前发现了这一事实。我不记得他的名字了。

segments.text: I'd have to look it up.
segments.translation: 我得查一下。

segments.text: Then other naturalists had the same idea - all independently of each other.
segments.translation: 然后其他博物学家也有同样的想法。他们的观点是相互独立的。

segments.text: MICHELLE: Oh right. We should talk about the immediate impact of the eruption, which was obviously enormous - especially in Iceland, where so many people died.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：哦，对了。我们应该谈谈火山喷发的直接影响，这显然是巨大的，尤其是在冰岛，那里有那么多人丧生。

segments.text: ADAM: Mmm. You'd expect that - and the fact that the volcanic ash drifted so swiftly - but not that the effects would go on for so long.
segments.translation: 亚当：嗯。人们能预测到火山灰会迅速飘散，但没有预计到的是这种影响会持续这么久。

segments.text: Or that two years after the eruption, strange weather events were being reported as far away as North America and North Africa.
segments.translation: 或者可以谈一下在火山喷发两年后，远至北美和北非都出现了奇怪的天气事件。

segments.text: MICHELLE: No. I found all that hard to believe too.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：是的。我也觉得难以置信。

segments.text: It must have been terrible - and there was nothing anyone could do about it, even if they knew the ash cloud was coming in their direction.
segments.translation: 这一定很可怕。而且即使他们知道火山灰云正朝他们的方向袭来，任何人都无能为力。

segments.text: MICHELLE: We should run through some of the terrible consequences of the eruption experienced in different countries.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：我们应该回顾一下不同国家经历的火山喷发的一些可怕后果。

segments.text: There's quite a varied range.
segments.translation: 范围很广。

segments.text: ADAM: Starting with Iceland, where the impact on farming was devastating.
segments.translation: 亚当：首先是冰岛，那里对农业的影响是毁灭性的。

segments.text: MICHELLE: Mmm. One of the most dramatic things there was the effect on livestock as they grazed in the fields.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：嗯。最引人注目的事情之一是对在田间吃草的牲畜的影响。

segments.text: They were poisoned because they ate vegetation that had been contaminated with fluorine as a result of the volcanic fallout.
segments.translation: 它们中毒是因为它们食用了因火山沉降物而被氟污染的植物。

segments.text: ADAM: That was horrible.
segments.translation: 亚当：那太可怕了。

segments.text: In Egypt, the bizarre weather patterns led to a severe drought and as a result the Nile didn't flood, which meant the crops all failed.
segments.translation: 在埃及，怪异的天气模式导致了严重的干旱，结果尼罗河没有泛滥，这意味着农作物全部欠收。

segments.text: MICHELLE: It's so far from where the eruption happened and yet the famine there led to more people dying than any other country.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：它离火山爆发的地方很远，但那里的饥荒导致的死亡人数比其他任何国家都多。

segments.text: It was worse than the plague.
segments.translation: 这比瘟疫还糟糕。

segments.text: ADAM: OK. Then in the UK the mortality rate went up a lot - presumably from respiratory illnesses.
segments.translation: 亚当：好的。然后在英国，死亡率上升了很多，大概是由于呼吸道疾病。

segments.text: According to one report it was about double the usual number and included an unusually high percentage of people under the age of 25.
segments.translation: 根据一份报告，这一数字大约是平时因该疾病死亡人数的两倍，其中包括异常高比例的25岁以下人群。

segments.text: MICHELLE: Mmm. I think people will be surprised to hear that the weather in the USA was badly affected too.
segments.translation: 米歇尔：嗯。我想人们听到美国的天气也受到严重影响时会感到惊讶。

segments.text: George Washington even makes a note in his diary that they were snowbound until March in Virginia.
segments.translation: 乔治·华盛顿甚至在他的日记中记下他们在弗吉尼亚州一直被大雪困到3月。

segments.text: That was before he became president.
segments.translation: 那是在他成为总统之前。

segments.text: ADAM: Yes, and there was ice floating down the Mississippi, which was unprecedented.
segments.translation: 亚当：是的，密西西比河上漂浮着冰，这是前所未有的。

segments.text: MICHELLE: Astonishing, really. Anyway, what do you think …
segments.translation: 米歇尔：真的很惊人。不管怎样，你怎么看……
